chunks.title: Introduction
chunks.content: This document provides essential information for healthcare professionals and patients regarding asthma management, focusing on inhaled corticosteroids and long-acting beta-agonists.
ICSs are the cornerstone of asthma treatment, effective for long-term control by reducing airway inflammation. Common side effects include oral thrush and potential impact on growth in children.
chunks.title: Medications: Inhaled Corticosteroids (ICS)
Medications: Long-Acting Beta2-Agonists (LABAs)
chunks.content: LABAs should only be used in combination with ICSs for asthma control. They carry a risk of severe exacerbations if used without adequate ICS.

chunks.title: Patient Education: Common Questions
chunks.content: Patients often have concerns about the safety and efficacy of ICSs. It is important to address these by emphasizing the benefits and the importance of proper usage.

chunks.title: Patient Education: Addressing Misconceptions
chunks.content: Clarify that ICSs are not addictive and are not the same as anabolic steroids used by athletes.

chunks.title: Resources
chunks.content: A list of organizations and websites for further information on asthma management and patient support.
